comment_text.sn1.20:1.3: At a later date, a deity in the same location asked Samiddhi if he knew the verses on “one fine night” (MN 133:1.3). Samiddhi further appears in discourses at MN 136, where he had gone forth only three years, and AN 9.14 , where he seems more mature. In SN 4.22, Māra threatens to shake his faith, prompting him to ask the Buddha about Māra’s true nature (SN 35.65). This event is recalled in his verse at Thag 1.46. The tale is expanded at Ja 167.
comment_text.sn1.20:1.5: The commentary says the deity is female, but he is addressed as āvuso by Samiddhi and yakkha by the Buddha, both of which are masculine.
comment_text.sn1.20:2.3: The deity is playing with two meanings of bhutvāna. The obvious sense is “having eaten”, because one can only eat after going for alms. By reversing the terms, they introduce a paradox—how can one eat without having obtained food?—and thereby force the second meaning of bhutvāna, “having enjoyed (the pleasures of life)”. They are suggesting that the mendicant should enjoy sensual pleasures while young, then go forth.
comment_text.sn1.20:3.1: Samiddhi joins the game, answering the deity with another play on words. He implies that “time” means the “time of death”.
comment_text.sn1.20:23.4: The third discrimination, omitted here, is “I am worse” (SN 45.162).
comment_text.sn1.20:25.1: Saṅkhā is sometimes used in a positive senses, as one who has “appraised” the world (sn7.20:3.3]()). Here, the context suggests it means “judgements” about people (DN 1:1.4.1, MN 18).| For na vimāna read na ca māna_ per Bodhi, Connected Discourses, note 38.

root_text.sn1.20:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati tapodārāme.
root_text.sn1.20:1.3: Atha kho āyasmā samiddhi rattiyā paccūsasamayaṁ paccuṭṭhāya yena tapodā tenupasaṅkami gattāni parisiñcituṁ.
root_text.sn1.20:1.4: Tapode gattāni parisiñcitvā paccuttaritvā ekacīvaro aṭṭhāsi gattāni pubbāpayamāno.
root_text.sn1.20:1.5: Atha kho aññatarā devatā abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ tapodaṁ obhāsetvā yena āyasmā samiddhi tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā vehāsaṁ ṭhitā āyasmantaṁ samiddhiṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn1.20:4.1: Atha kho sā devatā pathaviyaṁ patiṭṭhahitvā āyasmantaṁ samiddhiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn1.20:4.2: “daharo tvaṁ, bhikkhu, pabbajito susu kāḷakeso, bhadrena yobbanena samannāgato, paṭhamena vayasā, anikkīḷitāvī kāmesu.
root_text.sn1.20:5.1: “Na khvāhaṁ, āvuso, sandiṭṭhikaṁ hitvā kālikaṁ anudhāvāmi.
root_text.sn1.20:5.2: Kālikañca khvāhaṁ, āvuso, hitvā sandiṭṭhikaṁ anudhāvāmi.
root_text.sn1.20:5.3: Kālikā hi, āvuso, kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā; ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.sn1.20:5.4: Sandiṭṭhiko ayaṁ dhammo akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhī”ti.
root_text.sn1.20:6.1: “Kathañca, bhikkhu, kālikā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo?
root_text.sn1.20:6.2: Kathaṁ sandiṭṭhiko ayaṁ dhammo akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhī”ti?
root_text.sn1.20:7.1: “Ahaṁ kho, āvuso, navo acirapabbajito adhunāgato imaṁ dhammavinayaṁ.
root_text.sn1.20:7.3: Ayaṁ so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho rājagahe viharati tapodārāme.
root_text.sn1.20:7.5: Yathā te bhagavā byākaroti tathā naṁ dhāreyyāsī”ti.
root_text.sn1.20:8.1: “Na kho, bhikkhu, sukaro so bhagavā amhehi upasaṅkamituṁ, aññāhi mahesakkhāhi devatāhi parivuto.
root_text.sn1.20:8.2: Sace kho tvaṁ, bhikkhu, taṁ bhagavantaṁ upasaṅkamitvā etamatthaṁ puccheyyāsi, mayampi āgaccheyyāma dhammassavanāyā”ti.
root_text.sn1.20:8.3: “Evamāvuso”ti kho āyasmā samiddhi tassā devatāya paṭissutvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā samiddhi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn1.20:9.1: “Idhāhaṁ, bhante, rattiyā paccūsasamayaṁ paccuṭṭhāya yena tapodā tenupasaṅkamiṁ gattāni parisiñcituṁ.
root_text.sn1.20:9.2: Tapode gattāni parisiñcitvā paccuttaritvā ekacīvaro aṭṭhāsiṁ gattāni pubbāpayamāno.
root_text.sn1.20:9.3: Atha kho, bhante, aññatarā devatā abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ tapodaṁ obhāsetvā yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā vehāsaṁ ṭhitā imāya gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn1.20:11.1: Evaṁ vutte, ahaṁ, bhante, taṁ devataṁ gāthāya paccabhāsiṁ:
root_text.sn1.20:13.1: Atha kho, bhante, sā devatā pathaviyaṁ patiṭṭhahitvā maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn1.20:13.2: ‘daharo tvaṁ, bhikkhu, pabbajito susu kāḷakeso, bhadrena yobbanena samannāgato, paṭhamena vayasā, anikkīḷitāvī kāmesu.
root_text.sn1.20:14.1: Evaṁ vuttāhaṁ, bhante, taṁ devataṁ etadavocaṁ:
root_text.sn1.20:14.2: ‘na khvāhaṁ, āvuso, sandiṭṭhikaṁ hitvā kālikaṁ anudhāvāmi;
root_text.sn1.20:14.3: kālikañca khvāhaṁ, āvuso, hitvā sandiṭṭhikaṁ anudhāvāmi.
root_text.sn1.20:14.4: Kālikā hi, āvuso, kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā; ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.sn1.20:14.5: Sandiṭṭhiko ayaṁ dhammo akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhī’ti.
root_text.sn1.20:15.1: Evaṁ vutte, bhante, sā devatā maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn1.20:15.2: ‘kathañca, bhikkhu, kālikā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā; ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo?
root_text.sn1.20:15.3: Kathaṁ sandiṭṭhiko ayaṁ dhammo akāliko ehipassiko opaneyyiko paccattaṁ veditabbo viññūhī’ti?
root_text.sn1.20:16.1: Evaṁ vuttāhaṁ, bhante, taṁ devataṁ etadavocaṁ:
root_text.sn1.20:16.2: ‘ahaṁ kho, āvuso, navo acirapabbajito adhunāgato imaṁ dhammavinayaṁ, na tāhaṁ sakkomi vitthārena ācikkhituṁ.
root_text.sn1.20:16.3: Ayaṁ so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho rājagahe viharati tapodārāme.
root_text.sn1.20:16.5: Yathā te bhagavā byākaroti tathā naṁ dhāreyyāsī’ti.
root_text.sn1.20:17.1: Evaṁ vutte, bhante, sā devatā maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn1.20:17.2: ‘na kho, bhikkhu, sukaro so bhagavā amhehi upasaṅkamituṁ, aññāhi mahesakkhāhi devatāhi parivuto.
root_text.sn1.20:17.3: Sace kho tvaṁ, bhikkhu, taṁ bhagavantaṁ upasaṅkamitvā etamatthaṁ puccheyyāsi, mayampi āgaccheyyāma dhammassavanāyā’ti.
root_text.sn1.20:17.4: Sace, bhante, tassā devatāya saccaṁ vacanaṁ, idheva sā devatā avidūre”ti.
root_text.sn1.20:18.1: Evaṁ vutte, sā devatā āyasmantaṁ samiddhiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn1.20:19.1: Atha kho bhagavā taṁ devataṁ gāthāhi ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn1.20:21.4: Yena naṁ vajjā na tassa atthi;
root_text.sn1.20:22.1: “Na khvāhaṁ, bhante, imassa bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitassa vitthārena atthaṁ ājānāmi.
root_text.sn1.20:22.2: Sādhu me, bhante, bhagavā tathā bhāsatu yathāhaṁ imassa bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitassa vitthārena atthaṁ jāneyyan”ti.
root_text.sn1.20:24.1: “Imassapi khvāhaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitassa na vitthārena atthaṁ ājānāmi.
root_text.sn1.20:24.2: Sādhu me, bhante, bhagavā tathā bhāsatu yathāhaṁ imassa bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitassa vitthārena atthaṁ jāneyyan”ti.
root_text.sn1.20:25.5: Devā manussā idha vā huraṁ vā,
root_text.sn1.20:27.1: “Imassa khvāhaṁ, bhante, bhagavatā saṅkhittena bhāsitassa evaṁ vitthārena atthaṁ ājānāmi—

translation_text.sn1.20:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha in the Hot Springs Monastery.
translation_text.sn1.20:1.3: Then Venerable Samiddhi rose at the crack of dawn and went to the hot springs to bathe.
translation_text.sn1.20:1.4: When he had bathed and emerged from the water he stood in one robe drying his limbs.
translation_text.sn1.20:1.5: Then, late at night, a glorious deity, lighting up the entire hot springs, went up to Samiddhi, and, standing in the air, addressed him in verse:
translation_text.sn1.20:2.1: “Mendicant, you seek alms before you eat;
translation_text.sn1.20:2.2: you don’t seek alms after eating.
translation_text.sn1.20:2.3: But you should eat first, then seek alms:
translation_text.sn1.20:2.4: don’t let the time pass you by.”
translation_text.sn1.20:3.3: That’s why I seek alms before eating,
translation_text.sn1.20:3.4: so that the time may not pass me by!”
translation_text.sn1.20:4.1: Then that deity landed on the ground and said to Samiddhi,
translation_text.sn1.20:4.2: “You’ve gone forth while young, mendicant. With pristine black hair, you’re blessed with youth, in the prime of life, and you’ve never flirted with sensual pleasures.
translation_text.sn1.20:4.4: Don’t give up what is apparent in the present to chase after what takes effect over time.”
translation_text.sn1.20:5.2: I’m giving up what takes effect over time to chase after what is apparent in the present.
translation_text.sn1.20:5.3: For the Buddha has said that sensual pleasures take effect over time, with much suffering and distress, and they’re all the more full of drawbacks.
translation_text.sn1.20:5.4: But this teaching is apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves.”
translation_text.sn1.20:6.1: “But in what way, mendicant, has the Buddha said that sensual pleasures take effect over time, with much suffering and distress, and they’re all the more full of drawbacks?
translation_text.sn1.20:6.2: And how is this teaching apparent in the present life, immediately effective, inviting inspection, relevant, so that sensible people can know it for themselves?”
translation_text.sn1.20:7.1: “I’m junior, respectable sir, recently gone forth, newly come to this teaching and training.
translation_text.sn1.20:7.2: I’m not able to explain this in detail.
translation_text.sn1.20:7.3: But he, that Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha, is staying near Rājagaha in the Hot Springs Monastery.
translation_text.sn1.20:7.4: You should approach him and ask about this matter.
translation_text.sn1.20:7.5: And you should remember it in line with the Buddha’s answer.”
translation_text.sn1.20:8.1: “It’s not easy for us to approach the Buddha, as he is surrounded by other illustrious deities.
translation_text.sn1.20:8.2: If you go to the Buddha and ask him about this matter, we’ll come along and listen to the teaching.”
translation_text.sn1.20:8.3: “Yes, reverend,” Venerable Samiddhi replied. He went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened. Then he added:
translation_text.sn1.20:17.4: “Sir, if that deity spoke the truth, he’ll be close by.”
translation_text.sn1.20:18.1: When he had spoken, that deity said to Samiddhi,
translation_text.sn1.20:18.2: “Ask, mendicant, ask! For I have arrived.”
translation_text.sn1.20:19.1: Then the Buddha addressed the deity in verse:
translation_text.sn1.20:20.4: they fall under the yoke of Death.
translation_text.sn1.20:21.1: But having fully understood the communicable,
translation_text.sn1.20:21.4: by which they might be described.
translation_text.sn1.20:21.5: Tell me if you understand, spirit.”
translation_text.sn1.20:22.1: “I don’t understand the detailed meaning of the Buddha’s brief statement.
translation_text.sn1.20:22.2: Please teach me this matter so I can understand the detailed meaning.”
translation_text.sn1.20:23.2: special, or worse’, you’ll dispute about that.
translation_text.sn1.20:23.3: Unwavering in the face of the three discriminations,
translation_text.sn1.20:23.4: you’ll have no thought ‘I’m equal or special’.
translation_text.sn1.20:23.5: Tell me if you understand, spirit.”
translation_text.sn1.20:24.1: “I don’t understand the detailed meaning of the Buddha’s brief statement.
translation_text.sn1.20:24.2: Please teach me this matter so I can understand the detailed meaning.”
translation_text.sn1.20:25.1: “They gave up judgement without falling into conceit;
translation_text.sn1.20:25.2: craving for name and form was cut off right here.
translation_text.sn1.20:25.3: They cut the ties, untroubled, <j>with no need for hope.
translation_text.sn1.20:25.4: Though gods and humans search for them
translation_text.sn1.20:25.5: in this life or the next, they never find them,
translation_text.sn1.20:25.6: not in heaven nor in any abode.
translation_text.sn1.20:26.1: Tell me if you understand, spirit.”
translation_text.sn1.20:27.1: “This is how I understand the detailed meaning of the Buddha’s brief statement:
translation_text.sn1.20:28.1: You should never do anything bad
translation_text.sn1.20:28.2: by speech or mind or body in all the world.
translation_text.sn1.20:28.3: Having given up sensual pleasures, <j>mindful and aware,
translation_text.sn1.20:28.4: you shouldn’t keep doing  <j>what’s painful and pointless.”

variant_text.sn1.20:7.2: Na tāhaṁ → na khvāhaṁ (bj, pts1ed, pts2ed)

reference_text.sn1.20:8.1: bj13.20, dr15.13, ms12S1_72, pts-vp-pli1ed1.10, pts-vp-pli2ed1.21, sya15.14
